steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Find the circumference
steps.content: The radius is $1$, so the circumference is $$2\pi r=2\pi(1)=2\pi.$$

steps.title: Divide arc length by circumference
steps.content: The fraction of the circumference is $$\frac{\pi/3}{2\pi}=\frac{\pi}{3}\cdot\frac{1}{2\pi}=\frac{1}{6}.$$

steps.title: Confirm accepted forms
steps.content: The answer is 1/6. Decimal entries like $.1666$, $.1667$, $0.166$, or $0.167$ are also accepted. The fraction is less than one full circle because the arc is only a small part of the circumference.
